title: Mitrella beccarii
intro: Mitrella beccarii, also known as Beccari's mitrella, is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in the Pacific Islands, for its potential health benefits, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, thereby aiding in the treatment of various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Mitrella beccarii include alkaloids, phenolic compounds, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Mitrella beccarii is often prepared as a decoction or infused in hot water to make a tea, or used as a topical application to treat skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Mitrella beccarii belongs to the family Apocynaceae. This species is classified as Mitrella beccarii within the genus Mitrella. The genus Mitrella is part of the subfamily Rauvolfioideae. Mitrella beccarii is a flowering plant native to New Caledonia. It is an evergreen shrub or small tree.
